subject: Sociology
title: Functionalist Theory of Education
description: A quick, practical guide to Functionalist Theory of Education.

hook.text: Society is a machine and schools are a critical gear. Break the gear and the machine stops running.

buildup.text: Functionalists like Durkheim and Parsons saw education as serving essential social functions for stability.

discovery.text: Schools provide socialization, skill training, role allocation, and social integration across diverse groups.

twist.text: This view assumes schools work fairly for everyone. It ignores how education reproduces existing inequality.

climax.text: Functionalism explains why education exists but struggles to explain why it fails so many students.

punchline.text: Schools work — the question is for whom.

quiz.question: What do functionalists believe about education?
options: It fulfills essential functions like socialization and skill training
